chunks.text: When I was in middle school, I was bullied.
Going to the classroom was a horror.
When the alarm rang in the morning, tears would pour out.
The cause was so small that no one could guess.
At the school sports day, my team lost a relay match and finished last.
From there, a certain mate began to blame me
and gradually the atmosphere of the class changed.
Garbage was placed inside the interior of my desk.
My gym uniform was dumped into the toilet.
No one would speak to me.
When I referred the matter to a teacher, I was dismissed with
" It's merely teasing.
" I was too embarrassed to confess to my parents.
A sense of shame made me carry it alone.
One day, I simply could not go to school.
My mother at first showed anger, but seeing my face, she understood everything.
Working with the school, my homeroom teacher was changed.
Several fellow from the class came to apologize to me.
I still remember the weight of the words, " I am truly sorry.
Bullying eats at the heart of not only the victim
but also the one who bullies.
One of those who had bullied me contacted me years later.
" I have been regretting it all this time," he said.
The wound does not completely disappear.
But that experience made the person I am today.
A person who knows weakness can be kinder to another's weakness.
The experience of suffering becomes a force that can someday relieve someone else.
Now, as a teacher, I keep telling children, " You are not alone.
